name: the-age-of-napoleon
description: Will & Ariel Durant's sweeping history of the Napoleonic era — from the French Revolution to Waterloo, spanning Napoleon's rise, military genius, reforms, decline, and the civilization he shaped.
useCase: Understanding Napoleon's rise and fall, military strategy, the French Revolution, leadership and hubris, the intersection of power with art and philosophy, and the reforms that outlasted the empire.
knownRisks: The Durants wrote from a Western, humanist perspective — their interpretations may differ from revisionist historians
The book was published in 1975 — some scholarship has evolved since then
This is one volume of an 11-volume series — reading it alone gives an incomplete picture of the Durants' overall thesis
The military history sections may be less detailed than specialized works on the Napoleonic Wars
